content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Inspected the full branch state and changed implementation files. The new retry loop inside src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs looks internally consistent, but there are still two fix-now gaps in engine-level final_review availability handling: transient reviewer probe failures can still shrink/fail the live panel before the retry wrapper runs, and resume still converts transient probe blips into terminal drift.

## Amendments

1. High: final_review still bypasses the new per-member retry path during engine-side reviewer probing. In src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:9487-9512, an optional reviewer that hits a transient check_availability error is dropped from the panel immediately, before execute_final_review_panel/invoke_final_review_member can apply the new retry loop. If that transient drop takes the panel below quorum, src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:9524-9544 returns AppError::InsufficientPanelMembers, but that error is non-retryable (src/shared/error.rs:345-354; src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5842-5863), so a single transient optional-reviewer probe failure can still hard-fail final_review. Even when quorum survives, the stage now votes with a smaller panel than configured, which can change consensus from a temporary backend blip. This needs to use the same transient classification/retry path as actual reviewer invocations instead of treating transient probe failures as durable panel shrinkage.
   Rationale: The bead is specifically about retrying transient final_review reviewer failures. The current engine preflight still lets transient availability errors either remove reviewers or fail the stage before the new retry wrapper ever runs, so the acceptance criteria are not met on the real execute_run path.
2. Medium: resume still treats transient final_review backend probe failures as stage drift. In src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2250-2287, resume re-checks the arbiter and required reviewers with adapter.check_availability and converts any non-exhaustion failure into ResumeDriftFailure. That means a temporary network/probe blip during `run resume` prevents the failed final_review stage from resuming at all, even though the invocation path in src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs would now retry the same backend once execution starts. Optional reviewers are also silently dropped on resume by src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2265-2299 for non-exhaustion probe errors, so resume can change panel membership for transient reasons.
   Rationale: The motivating bug report explicitly involved succeeding after `run resume`. Declaring transient final_review probe failures as resume drift leaves the recovery path brittle and still outside the new retry logic.
